purpose: Chain-row layout for swarms whose tasks form linear dependency chains. Renders one header row of wave cells across the top, then one row per chain. Each chain cell at column `wave` shows the task in that chain that belongs to that wave. Uses ChainWaveHeader (wave title + StatusBadge) for headers and AgentCard for the body. Filters cells by `activeStatFilter` and skips entire chains when nothing is visible. Note: dependency-line SVG overlay is intentionally omitted (per file comment, deferred to a follow-up).

exports.name: ChainPipeline
exports.signature: { status: { agents[], waves[], chains[] }, activeStatFilter: string|null, onAgentClick: (agent) => void }

gotchas: Assumes each chain has at most one task per wave — the `for...break` loop picks the first match. Plans that violate this silently lose tasks.
Empty chain rows are pruned only when activeStatFilter is set; without a filter, chains with no agents in some columns still render an empty cell.
STATUS_COLORS / STATUS_BG_COLORS are imported but unused at this layer; only StatusBadge consumes them downstream.
patterns: Lookup map (agentMap) built once per render to avoid quadratic scans inside nested loops.
VisibleIds Set computed once when filter is active so chain pruning is O(chains).
conventions: Uses chain.id and wave.id as React keys; chain.tasks is an array of task ids (strings).
